Unnamed: 9: Unemployment is unique case because directional variable is -1 but its average is negative

In this case the actual was less forecast and the double negative makes it positive but the directional variable makes it negative again even though this lower than expected unemployment is positive for the macro environment

Because of this im wondering if for surprises we should do (Actual - Forecast)/Sdev instead of using the actual stats z score formula because of what it is exactly we want to have the model take into account.

With the regular z scoring it would measure more so how efficient markets are or how well they are pricing information, meaning that worse than expected CPI can end up being positive if the difference is less than the average
